title: Publish Gates Manifest v1
description: Declares the gates a Brain runs before publishing. Read by `neurogrim publish-gate run` (S12-G-4) and validated by `neurogrim doctor` (S12-G-3). Lives at `<brain>/.claude/brain/publish-gates.yaml`. Schema is closed-vocabulary at every level (`additionalProperties: false`) — operators MAY extend by spec change with explicit METHODOLOGY-EVOLUTION entry, MUST NOT silently extend in adopter manifests. Three gate types are supported: `automated` (the runner executes `check_command` and treats exit 0 as pass), `manual` (the runner prints `instructions` and waits for operator y/n), `e2e` (the runner invokes the Playwright harness; bounded by `timeout_seconds`).

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. v1 is the only valid value today; future bumps require a new schema sidecar (`publish-gates-v2.schema.json`) and a doctor finding when an old manifest is read by a newer binary.

gates.description: Ordered list of gate declarations. Order is significant — the runner executes automated gates in declared order so adopters can sequence cheap-fast gates before expensive-slow ones (e.g., lint → test → e2e).

id.description: Stable, kebab-case identifier. Used as the ledger primary key in `publish-gate-ledger.jsonl` (S12-G-4) and as the `--gate <id>` argument when running a single gate. MUST be unique within the manifest — duplicate IDs are rejected by `neurogrim doctor`.

gate_type.description: Closed vocabulary. `automated` = runner executes `check_command`, exit 0 = pass; `manual` = runner prints `instructions` and prompts operator (recorded with `$NEUROGRIM_OPERATOR`); `e2e` = runner invokes the Playwright harness (S12-G-5). Adding a new type requires a v2 schema bump.

description.description: Human-readable one-liner. Surfaces in `publish-gate run` output and in the dashboard's `/brains/:id/publish-gates` page.

blocking.description: If true (default), a failing gate stops the publish pipeline. If false, the gate is advisory — failure is logged but does not exit non-zero. v4.0 ships with default-on hard gates per the 2026-04-29 single-adopter decision; advisory mode is the safety valve when a gate is being authored / tuned.

timeout_seconds.description: Wall-clock timeout for the gate. Capped at 1 hour to prevent a runaway gate from blocking a publish indefinitely. The runner kills the gate process on timeout and records a `timed_out` ledger entry.

check_command.description: Shell command for `automated` gates. Executed via `sh -c` on Unix and `cmd /c` on Windows. Inherits the publish-gate runner's cwd (project root) and environment. Required for `automated` gates; ignored for `manual` and `e2e`.

instructions.description: Multi-line operator-facing instructions for `manual` gates. Markdown-rendered in the dashboard surface (S12-G-6). Required for `manual` gates.

operator_required.description: For `manual` gates: whether `$NEUROGRIM_OPERATOR` must be set before the gate can be marked passed. Default true matches the audit-trail discipline the spec requires for governance. Setting false is an explicit opt-out (e.g., for unattended local dev where the operator handle isn't meaningful).
